mcp.title: MCP Overview

mcp_aws_sigv4.description: Use AWS SigV4 authentication to connect LiteLLM to MCP servers hosted on AWS Bedrock AgentCore.

mcp_control.title: MCP Permission Management
mcp_control.description: Control which MCP servers and tools can be accessed by specific keys, teams, or organizations in LiteLLM. When a client attempts to list or call tools, LiteLLM enforces access controls based on configured permissions.

mcp_cost.title: MCP Cost Tracking
mcp_cost.description: LiteLLM provides two ways to track costs for MCP tool calls:

mcp_guardrail.title: MCP Guardrails
mcp_guardrail.description: LiteLLM supports applying guardrails to MCP tool calls to ensure security and compliance. You can configure guardrails to run before or during MCP calls to validate inputs and block or mask sensitive information.

mcp_oauth.title: MCP OAuth
mcp_oauth.description: LiteLLM supports two OAuth 2.0 flows for MCP servers:

mcp_openapi.title: MCP from OpenAPI Specs
mcp_openapi.description: LiteLLM can convert any OpenAPI/Swagger spec into an MCP server — no custom MCP server code required.

mcp_public_internet.title: Exposing MCPs on the Public Internet
mcp_public_internet.description: Control which MCP servers are visible to external callers (e.g., ChatGPT, Claude Desktop) vs. internal-only callers. This is useful when you want a subset of your MCP servers available publicly while keeping sensitive servers restricted to your private network.

mcp_semantic_filter.title: MCP Semantic Tool Filter
mcp_semantic_filter.description: Automatically filter MCP tools by semantic relevance. When you have many MCP tools registered, LiteLLM semantically matches the user's query against tool descriptions and sends only the most relevant tools to the LLM.

mcp_toolsets.title: MCP Toolsets
mcp_toolsets.description: A Toolset is a named collection of specific tools drawn from one or more MCP servers. Instead of giving an agent access to every tool on every server, you pick exactly which tools it needs — from whichever servers they live on — and bundle them under a single name.

mcp_troubleshoot.title: MCP Troubleshooting Guide
mcp_troubleshoot.description: When LiteLLM acts as an MCP proxy, traffic normally flows Client → LiteLLM Proxy → MCP Server, while OAuth-enabled setups add an authorization server for metadata discovery.
